question_text: A student observes that when sodium hydroxide solution is added to hydrochloric acid, the beaker becomes warm. Which statement correctly evaluates this observation?

options.label: A
options.text: This is an endothermic physical change because the temperature increases

options.label: B
options.text: This is an exothermic chemical change because new substances form and heat energy is released

options.label: C
options.text: This is a reversible change because heat is produced and can be removed

options.label: D
options.text: This is an endothermic chemical change because the beaker temperature increases above room temperature

explanation: Neutralisation is a chemical reaction forming new substances (salt and water). The temperature increase shows energy is released to surroundings, making it exothermic. Endothermic reactions absorb heat and cool surroundings, so option A and D are incorrect.
